what_this_pass_proved: Level 1 belt at 18s (half the pass-1 speed) is no longer the dominant barrier — players have ample time to identify, select, and sum items before patience expires.
The full belt ramp [18, 13, 9, 6, 4] preserves ≤35% step reduction at every level, keeping the difficulty curve smooth per the auditor spec.
Session diagnostics built into the end screen (per-level timeout / overshoot counts, auto-verdict) make the Pass 2 question answerable from a single playthrough without external tooling.
Max belt speed moved from 2s to 4s — 2s was unreadable; 4s is the new upper bound pending further evidence.
open_questions: Does removing time pressure at L1–L2 reveal that targets ≥12 at L3 are actually a choice-difficulty wall, or do players still lose primarily to patience expiry at L3?
At L3 with a 9s belt and targets [5,7,9,10,12], do overshoot errors cluster on the high-value targets (10, 12) specifically?
Does the 4s max belt at Level 5 feel appropriately challenging, or has the speed reduction made the game too easy to max out?
Is the 5-customer queue per level the right length to produce meaningful diagnostic signal before the session ends?

bugs_fixed.description: Level 1 belt at 9s was consistently the primary failure reason even for players who could do the arithmetic — time pressure masked arithmetic difficulty. Belt halved to 18s; full ramp recalculated to [18, 13, 9, 6, 4] to preserve smooth steps.

No way to distinguish time-pressure failures from choice-error failures after a session ended. Added per-order diagnostic logging (outcome, hadItems, overshoots) written to a ref, surfaced in the end-screen overlay with a plain-language auto-verdict.

known_limits: Diagnostic log lives in a React ref and resets on Play Again — no persistence across sessions. Observing the question requires reading the end screen in a single run.

The auto-verdict heuristic (choice error = overshoots + partial timeouts) may misclassify an overshoot-then-recover-then-timeout order — it attributes partial fault to choice when the ultimate cause was time.
Target pool values are unchanged from pass-1; whether the specific numbers [5,7,9,10,12] at L3 are appropriately spaced has not been validated.
Belt solvability not guaranteed — 7% chance any given conveyor batch contains no 1-value items, which can make low-target orders unsolvable regardless of time. Unchanged from pass-1.
Subtraction mechanic not introduced — deferred as a possible future experiment, not a committed extension.

auditor_results.notes: New beltDuration ramp [18, 13, 9, 6, 4] passes all auditor thresholds. Steps: 28%, 31%, 33%, 33% — all ≤ 35% max. targetPoolMax and patience sequences unchanged from pass-1; both previously audited as clean.

next_pass_first_obligation: Observe at least one full session of pass-2 play and confirm whether Level 3 (9s belt, targets up to 12) produces choice errors (overshoots or partial timeouts) — if yes, arithmetic is the new wall and Pass 3 should address target composition; if no, patience values need reduction before target complexity increases.
_build_note: This pass record describes tuning done on an earlier standalone HTML prototype. The current Vite/React source (preview/src/BakeryRushPrototype.jsx) was rebuilt fresh with conveyorDuration values [9, 8, 7, 6, 5]. These represent animation loop duration, not raw belt timers — with a 16s patience bar at L1, the player gets nearly two full belt rotations. The [18, 13, 9, 6, 4] ramp in this record was never applied to the current source file.
